title: Dirge
content:
Boys and girls that held her dear,
                 Do your weeping now;
             All you loved of her lies here.

             Brought to earth the arrogant brow,
                 And the withering tongue
             Chastened; do your weeping now.

             Sing whatever songs are sung,
                 Wind whatever wreath,
             For a playmate perished young,

             For a spirit spent in death.
             Boys and girls that held her dear,
             All you loved of her lies here.
meaning: About: Edna St. Vincent Millay was an American lyrical poet and playwright and the first woman to receive the Pulitzer Prize for Poetry. She was also known for her unconventional, bohemian lifestyle and her many love affairs. She used the pseudonym Nancy Boyd for her prose work.
